name: Roof Replacement
card_summary: Full tear-off and rebuild. Architectural asphalt, standing seam metal, concrete tile, DaVinci synthetic slate. Every roofing system Central Texas climate is rated for, installed by the same family-built crew that has worked Austin for twenty-seven years.
direct_answer: A full roof replacement in Austin runs $8,000 to $18,000 for architectural asphalt shingle on a typical 2,000 square foot home in 2026, $14,000 to $28,000 for standing seam metal, and $24,000 to $48,000 for concrete tile or DaVinci synthetic slate. Austin Area Roofers tears the old system off to the decking, inspects and replaces decking where it has failed, then installs the new system in one to three days for most homes.
intro_paragraphs: Twenty-seven years of Austin roof replacements means we have already seen what your house is hiding under the layers. Soft decking from the 2009 ice storm. Skip-sheathing on the 1920s Tarrytown and Pemberton Heights bungalows. Hail-bruised felt from the 2024 Travis County storms that hit Round Rock, Cedar Park, and Pflugerville hardest. Every replacement starts with a full deck inspection. Every quote names what we find on it.

We install every system Central Texas climate is rated for. Architectural asphalt shingle for the workhorse install at the $8,000 to $18,000 range. Standing seam metal in 22-gauge G90 galvalume for the modern build and the long-haul homeowner. Concrete and clay tile for the Hill Country aesthetic on Westlake Hills and Bee Cave custom builds. DaVinci synthetic slate for the historic look on Tarrytown and Old Enfield restorations without the historic weight load. We hold the master tier with IKO, Decra, and DaVinci. Few crews in the Austin metro carry all three credentials.

Every install is the same family-built crew. No franchise teams. No subcontracted strangers on your property. Benny walks every estimate himself and is on every job site. The reason most Austin roofs that fail in year ten are because somebody else cut a corner on flashing or skipped the decking inspection. That does not happen here.

what_we_do.p: Old shingle, old underlayment, old flashing all stripped. Every layer leaves in our dumpster, not on your driveway. Magnetic sweepers run twice across the lawn and beds before we leave.

Soft, rotten, or hail-bruised decking gets replaced before the new system goes on top of it. We price it transparently if we find it and show you the photos. Skip-sheathing on historic Tarrytown and Old Enfield homes gets handled to current Austin code.

what_we_do.h: Synthetic underlayment, ice and water shield, drip edge
what_we_do.p: Synthetic underlayment on every elevation. Ice and water shield laid in valleys and around every penetration. Drip edge on every eave and rake. Current Austin code on every job, regardless of which Central Texas city the home sits in.

what_we_do.h: New system installed to manufacturer spec
what_we_do.p: Architectural shingle, snap-lock standing seam, concealed-fastener metal, concrete tile, clay tile, DaVinci synthetic slate. You pick the system from samples on your driveway. We install it to the exact manufacturer specification that makes the full warranty register.

Final inspection signed by two crew leads. Photos to your file. Manufacturer warranty registered in your name before our truck leaves the property. We do not leave the warranty for the homeowner to chase.

process_steps.p: Sixty seconds online or a five-minute call. We size the roof from satellite imagery and give you a range accurate within eight percent before anyone steps on your property.
Benny or a certified Austin Area Roofers inspector walks every slope, checks the attic from inside, photographs every elevation, and writes the report the same day. Free and no obligation.
Samples on your driveway. Full IKO, Decra, DaVinci, and Owens Corning catalogues. We do not sell up. We install what fits the house, the budget, and the Central Texas climate it sits in.
We pull the permit with the City of Austin, Round Rock, Cedar Park, or whichever municipality applies. We schedule around your work week and deliver materials the day before the crew arrives.

Same family-built crew start to finish. No franchise teams cycling in and out. Final magnetic sweep, manufacturer warranty registered, two-year workmanship warranty written. We are done in one to three days for most Austin homes.

faqs.q: How much does a roof replacement cost in Austin in 2026?
faqs.a: A complete asphalt shingle roof replacement in Austin runs $8,000 to $18,000 for a typical 2,000 to 2,500 square foot home. Standing seam metal in 22-gauge G90 galvalume runs $14,000 to $28,000. Concrete tile, clay tile, and DaVinci synthetic slate run $24,000 to $48,000. The average Austin home with architectural shingle lands at $12,000 to $15,000 installed. Final price depends on roof pitch, complexity, decking condition, and which manufacturer line you choose.

faqs.q: How long does a roof replacement take in Austin?
faqs.a: Most Austin asphalt shingle roofs install in one to two days. Standing seam metal runs two to four days because every panel is cut to length on site. Concrete and clay tile take three to five days. DaVinci synthetic slate takes three to five days. Weather, decking repair, and complex multi-elevation geometry can extend the schedule. We tell you the day count at the quote and we hit it.

faqs.q: Will my Austin homeowners insurance cover the replacement?
faqs.a: If the damage is from a covered storm event such as hail, wind, or fallen debris, most Texas homeowners insurance covers a roof replacement minus your deductible. We file the supplements, photograph every damage point in 10 square foot test squares, and meet your State Farm, Allstate, USAA, Farmers, or other adjuster on the roof. About eighty percent of our hail damage replacements close insurance-covered minus deductible.

faqs.q: Do I need to be home during the install?
faqs.a: No. We coordinate driveway and gate access ahead of time, work around your routine, and you can leave for work or run errands. We text you photos at every milestone and call before we leave the property. If you would rather be present, that is welcome too.

faqs.q: What happens to my landscaping, gutters, AC unit, and pool?
faqs.a: Heavy tarps cover every flower bed, AC condenser, pool, hot tub, and outdoor furniture the night before. Plywood goes against any glass or solar panels. Magnetic sweepers run at mid-install and at close-out so no nails get left behind. Gutters get debris-cleaned as part of the price. If we damage anything, we fix it on our dime.

faqs.q: What warranty comes with the new roof?
faqs.a: Manufacturer warranty runs 25 to 50 years on materials depending on the system, lifetime on the premium DaVinci and Decra tiers. Our written workmanship warranty runs ten years on labor and is transferable once to the next owner. Both are registered in your name the day we finish and emailed to you before we leave the property.

faqs.q: Why is Austin Area Roofers different from the big franchise roofers?
faqs.a: No franchise. No salesmen. No subcontracted strangers. The same family-built crew that has worked the Austin metro for twenty-seven years walks every estimate and runs every install. Benny is on every job site. Master tier certifications on IKO, Decra, and DaVinci. Few crews in Central Texas hold all three. That is the entire difference.

name: Roof Repair
card_summary: Leaks traced to the actual source. Flashings rebuilt, ridge caps replaced, pipe boots reset. Two-year workmanship warranty on every repair, written before our truck leaves your driveway.
direct_answer: Most Austin roof repairs run $450 to $1,800 depending on access, materials, and the extent of the damage. A cracked pipe boot is $350 to $550. A chimney flashing rebuild is $550 to $1,100. A shingle and underlayment section replacement is $800 to $2,000. Austin Area Roofers carries a same-week repair guarantee for non-emergency leaks and offers emergency tarp service within 24 hours during storm season. Every repair carries a two-year workmanship warranty written on close-out.
intro_paragraphs: Most Austin homeowners call us with the same opening line. They think they have a leak. Sometimes they do. Sometimes the stain on the ceiling came from a plumbing line above it, a window seal that failed, or condensation off an AC return run inside the wall. Twenty-seven years of repair calls in the Austin metro means we know how to trace the leak to the actual source before we quote the fix.

We repair every system we install. Architectural shingle, snap-lock standing seam, concealed-fastener metal, concrete tile, clay tile, DaVinci synthetic slate. We carry matching colors of IKO, Decra, and Owens Corning shingle lines on the truck so the repair is not visible from the street. Flashing repairs, valley repairs, ridge cap replacement, pipe boot reseals, isolated decking replacement, and matched-section shingle work all sit inside the same crew that does our full replacements.

We will also tell you honestly when a repair makes economic sense to chase and when it does not. About fifteen percent of the repair calls we run end in our recommending a full replacement instead because the roof is past the point where another four-figure repair throws good money after bad. We will give you that read straight and let you decide.

what_we_do.h: Leak diagnosis to the actual source
what_we_do.p: We trace the leak path. Water often travels along a rafter or down a sheathing seam before showing up six feet from the entry point on the ceiling. No guesswork, no roof reseal-everything pitch, no charging you for the wrong fix.

Most Austin leaks are flashing failures, not shingle failures. Chimney step flashing, sidewall counter flashing, valley metal, and skylight flashings are the spots that fail first in Central Texas heat-and-cold cycles. We rebuild them by hand on the truck.

Matched material from our stocked truck inventory. We do not patch with a different color and call it done. If the matched color is unavailable in the original line, we replace the full slope so the roof reads as one age from the street.
Sun-cracked pipe boots are the most common Austin roof leak, period. Ridge cap replacement on hail-bruised sections. Lead boots on the higher-end builds. Same-day service on most pipe boot jobs.
Storm hit overnight and you have water in the house? We tarp the roof the same day so your insurance has the time it needs to send an adjuster. No charge for the tarp if we end up doing the repair.

process_steps.p: Same-week scheduling for non-emergencies. Within 24 hours for active leaks during storm season. Phone answered through to the close, no answering service.
An Austin Area Roofers inspector walks every slope, checks the attic from inside, photographs the source of the leak, and emails the findings the same day. Free and no obligation.
Repair, replacement, or both, with a recommendation. We will tell you straight when a repair is throwing good money after bad and when a planned replacement is the better economic call.
Most repairs are single-visit and same week. Larger jobs scheduled within fourteen days. Photos sent before, during, and after. Site cleaned with magnetic sweepers before we leave.
Every repair carries a two-year workmanship warranty. If the same spot leaks again, we come back and fix it at no charge. Written on close-out.

faqs.q: Should I repair or replace my Austin roof?
faqs.a: Repair is the right call when damage is localized, the rest of the system is sound, and your roof is under fifteen years old. Repair if you have a few cracked pipe boots, an isolated leak, flashing that has separated, or storm damage limited to one slope. Replace if you have multiple leak points, widespread granule loss across every slope, the roof is over eighteen years old, or confirmed hail damage spanning more than twenty-five percent of the surface. We give you the honest answer at the free inspection.

faqs.q: How much does a roof repair cost in Austin?
faqs.a: Most Austin roof repairs land between $450 and $1,800. A cracked pipe boot reseal runs $350 to $550. A chimney flashing rebuild runs $550 to $1,100. A section of shingles plus underlayment runs $800 to $2,000. Complex valley repairs or large chimney flashings can run higher. We quote firm in writing before any work starts.

faqs.q: How fast can you fix a leak in Austin?
faqs.a: Same week for most non-emergencies. Within 24 hours for active leaks during storm season. If a storm is forecast and you have an exposed leak, we tarp the roof the same day so insurance has the time it needs to send the adjuster, then return for the permanent repair.

faqs.q: Will my Austin homeowners insurance cover the repair?
faqs.a: If the damage is from a covered storm event, often yes, minus your deductible. Wear-and-tear repairs are usually not covered because they fall under maintenance. We document the damage with photos, file the supplement, meet your adjuster on the roof, and walk you through the process honestly. Most Austin Area Roofers storm-damage repairs close insurance-covered after deductible.

faqs.q: Do you guarantee your repair work?
faqs.a: Two-year workmanship warranty on every repair, written on close-out. If the same spot leaks again inside that window, we return and fix it at no charge. We also stand behind the manufacturer warranty on any replaced materials.

faqs.q: What is the most common roof leak in Austin?
faqs.a: Cracked pipe boots, by a wide margin. Central Texas sun degrades the rubber gasket on standard pipe boots in eight to twelve years even though the shingle around them still has fifteen to twenty years of life left. Second most common is chimney flashing failure. Both are repairable for under $700 in most cases.

name: Metal Roofing
card_summary: Snap-lock and concealed-fastener standing seam in 22-gauge G90 galvalume. Class 4 impact rated. Eligible for a ten to twenty percent Texas homeowners insurance discount on the wind and hail portion of the premium.
direct_answer: Standing seam metal roofing in Austin runs $14,000 to $28,000 installed on a typical 2,000 square foot home with a 40 to 50 year service life. The Class 4 impact rating qualifies most Texas homeowners insurance policies for a ten to twenty percent discount on the wind and hail portion of the annual premium. Austin Area Roofers installs Decra stone-coated steel, snap-lock standing seam in 22-gauge G90 galvalume, and concealed-fastener systems for both residential and light commercial.
intro_paragraphs: Metal is the longest-life roof Central Texas climate is rated for. Forty to fifty years on a properly installed standing seam system. Class 4 impact rated against the hail Austin sees three to five times a year across Travis and Williamson Counties. UV-stable at the 150 degree Fahrenheit roof surface temperatures we see in July and August. And quietly, a ten to twenty percent homeowners insurance discount on most Texas carriers for the impact rating alone.

We install three metal systems. Snap-lock standing seam in 22-gauge G90 galvalume in PVDF Kynar 500 finishes that are rated thirty-five years before significant paint fade. Concealed-fastener systems where no screw penetrates the panel face, which eliminates the most common leak point on cheaper metal installs. Decra stone-coated steel in Tilcor Antique and Heritage profiles that match the look of tile, shingle, or shake with the lifespan and impact rating of metal.

We are certified Decra installers. That matters more than most homeowners realize. The Decra warranty requires a certified installer for the full fifty-year coverage to register. We are also one of two or three crews in the Austin metro that still hand-fold, hand-snap, and hand-weld the standing seam transitions at the ridge, hip, and valley. Most Austin metal jobs are subcontracted out at the transitions. Ours are not.

what_we_do.p: G90 galvalume base metal with PVDF Kynar 500 finish. Panels cut to length on your driveway, not at a factory. Clips concealed under the snap-lock seam so no fastener penetrates the panel face.

The standard for premium installs. Every fastener hidden under the next panel. Zero face penetration. Zero opportunity for the rubber gasket on an exposed screw to fail and start a leak in year twelve.

Looks like tile, shingle, or shake. Fifty-year manufacturer warranty. Class 4 impact rated. We hold the Decra Certified Installer credential, which is required for the full warranty to register on the home.
Commercial standing seam, R-panel for warehouses and outbuildings, snap-lock for residential. Full commercial systems with full manufacturer warranties. We do not subcontract commercial out.

We hand-deliver the Class 4 impact rating documentation for your insurance carrier the day we finish. Most Austin policies honor a ten to twenty percent discount on the wind and hail portion of the premium with this letter on file.

process_steps.p: We size the roof from satellite, walk you through the three metal systems with samples on your driveway, and give you firm written pricing on each before committing.
Most standing seam panels are cut to length on your driveway for your specific roof dimensions. No factory waste, no field seams where two panels meet, no leaks where they would have been.
Full tear-off to decking. Decking inspection and replacement where needed. Synthetic underlayment, ice and water shield in valleys and at every penetration, drip edge on every eave.
Two to four days for a residential standing seam. Clips, panels, ridges, hips, gable ends, valleys all to manufacturer specification. Hand-welded transitions on the complex Hill Country geometry.
We hand you the Class 4 impact rating documentation in writing the day we finish. Most Texas carriers process the discount within thirty days of receiving it.

faqs.q: How much does a metal roof cost in Austin?
faqs.a: Standing seam metal in 22-gauge G90 galvalume runs $14,000 to $28,000 installed on a typical 2,000 square foot Austin home. Decra stone-coated steel runs $18,000 to $32,000. Commercial R-panel runs $9 to $14 per square foot installed. The cost premium over architectural asphalt is typically recovered in twelve to eighteen years through the insurance discount and the avoided replacement, then you have twenty-five to thirty-five years of zero additional roof spend.

faqs.q: How long does a metal roof last in Central Texas?
faqs.a: Forty to fifty years for a properly installed snap-lock or concealed-fastener standing seam system in 22-gauge G90 galvalume. Decra stone-coated steel carries a fifty-year manufacturer warranty. The PVDF Kynar 500 paint finish is rated thirty-five years before significant fade. We have standing seam roofs we installed in Austin in 1998 that have outlived two coats of paint on the house underneath.

faqs.q: Does a metal roof qualify for a Texas homeowners insurance discount?
faqs.a: Yes. Class 4 impact-rated metal qualifies for a ten to twenty percent discount on the wind and hail portion of the homeowners premium on every major Texas carrier including USAA, State Farm, Allstate, Farmers, Liberty Mutual, Travelers, and most regional carriers. We document the rating in writing and hand you the letter on the day we finish so you can submit it to your carrier.

faqs.q: Is a metal roof noisy when it rains in Austin?
faqs.a: No, not with a modern install. The decking, the synthetic underlayment, and the attic insulation between the panels and your ceiling mean a properly installed Austin metal roof sounds about the same as architectural asphalt shingle in a Central Texas thunderstorm. The noise myth comes from old uninsulated barn-roof systems with no underlayment, which is a different product.

faqs.q: Does a metal roof attract lightning?
faqs.a: No. Every insurance industry study shows metal roofs are no more likely to be struck by lightning than any other roof type. Lightning seeks the highest grounded point, not the most conductive surface. Metal roofs are also non-combustible, so if lightning does strike, the roof does not ignite the way an asphalt shingle slope might.

faqs.q: Will a metal roof survive Austin hail?
faqs.a: Standing seam metal and Decra stone-coated steel both carry Class 4 impact ratings, which is the highest available. Class 4 Austin metal roofs typically survive hail events that take out architectural asphalt roofs on the same block. We have replaced asphalt roofs and left the neighboring metal roof untouched on the same storm event in Cedar Park and Round Rock.

name: Hail Damage Repair
h1: Hail Damage Repair in Austin, TX
card_summary: Free hail inspection within 48 hours. Damage documented in 10 square foot test squares. We meet your State Farm, Allstate, USAA, or Farmers adjuster on the roof. About eighty percent of our hail replacements close insurance-covered after deductible.
direct_answer: Hail damage to an Austin roof typically requires a full replacement if more than eight hits per 10 square foot test square are confirmed by an adjuster, which most Texas insurance carriers pay for after deductible. Austin Area Roofers runs free hail inspections within 48 hours of any confirmed Travis or Williamson County hail event, photographs every hit for the claim file, meets the adjuster on the roof, and files supplements when items are missed. About eighty percent of our hail damage replacements close insurance-covered after the homeowner's deductible.
intro_paragraphs: Austin sits in the heart of the Central Texas hail belt. Three to five hail events cross the metro every year. The April 2024 Travis County storms produced 2.5 to 4 inch stones across north Austin, Round Rock, Cedar Park, Pflugerville, and Georgetown. Most architectural asphalt roofs in those neighborhoods have hail damage. Most homeowners do not know it until water shows up on the ceiling six to twelve months later, when the insurance window has already closed.

Twenty-seven years of hail claims means we know exactly what insurance adjusters look for and what they miss. We do not chase storms. We do not knock doors. We do not promise a free roof to get the signed contract before we have even seen the property. We inspect honestly, photograph every hit in standard 10 square foot test squares per slope, build the claim file, and meet your adjuster on the roof so what should be covered actually gets covered. About eighty percent of our hail damage replacements close insurance-covered minus the homeowner's deductible.

We work directly with every major Texas carrier. State Farm, Allstate, USAA, Farmers, Liberty Mutual, Travelers, Nationwide, and every regional carrier in Central Texas. When a carrier misses items at the initial inspection, which happens regularly on code upgrades, decking, drip edge, and ice and water shield, we file supplements with full photographic documentation. Most supplements approve. The homeowner pays the original deductible and nothing more.

what_we_do.p: Within 48 hours of a confirmed hail event in your Austin zip code. No obligation. No door-knocking. No high-pressure pitch. You call us when you are ready and we are on the roof the next available appointment.

Every hit photographed in standard 10 square foot test squares per slope, the same way adjusters measure. Granule loss, mat bruising, soft spots, exposed asphalt, and damage to soft metals like vents and gutters all documented for the claim file.

We help you file the claim with your carrier, walk you through the language they expect to hear, and prepare the file with photos before the adjuster shows up. We do not file claims on your behalf because the carrier requires the homeowner-of-record, but we shorten the process to fifteen minutes.

Whichever day your carrier sends the adjuster, we are on the roof with them. This is the single highest-leverage step in any hail damage claim. An adjuster left alone on the roof for forty-five minutes will sometimes miss the damage another adjuster would have caught.

what_we_do.h: Supplement filing when items are missed
what_we_do.p: When adjusters miss code upgrades, decking, drip edge, ice and water shield, or specific damage areas, we file supplements with full photographic documentation. Most supplements approve. The homeowner stays at the original deductible.

Full tear-off to deck. Decking replacement where needed. Current Austin code on underlayment, ice and water shield, and drip edge. Class 4 impact-rated upgrade options on every quote so the next storm does this less.

process_steps.p: Call us or book online after any confirmed hail event in your zip. We are on the roof within 48 hours. Free regardless of outcome.
Photo report delivered same day with our findings. Confirmed damage or not. Honest answer either way. About thirty percent of post-storm inspections we run conclude the roof is fine.
We help you file the claim with State Farm, Allstate, USAA, Farmers, or whoever holds your policy. We prepare the file in the format adjusters expect.
Whichever day the carrier sends their adjuster, we are on the roof with them. This is the most important fifteen minutes of the entire claim.
Once the claim is approved, replacement is one to three days. You pay your deductible. We bill the carrier directly for the rest. Class 4 impact-rated upgrade options offered on every quote.

faqs.q: How do I know if my Austin roof has hail damage?
faqs.a: Visible signs include dented soft metals like vents, gutters, AC fins, and downspouts, shingles with circular bruises or missing granules in patches, and exposed asphalt mat where the granules have been knocked off. Many hail-damaged Austin roofs look fine from the ground. A trained inspector can confirm or rule out damage in a fifteen-minute roof walk. We do these inspections free across the Austin metro within 48 hours of any confirmed hail event.

faqs.q: Does my Austin homeowners insurance cover hail damage?
faqs.a: Almost every Texas homeowners policy covers hail damage to the roof subject to your deductible. The carrier sends an adjuster, who confirms the damage by counting hits in 10 square foot test squares. If eight or more hits per square are confirmed, most carriers approve a full replacement. We help you file, document, and meet the adjuster on the roof. About eighty percent of our hail damage replacements close insurance-covered minus the deductible.

faqs.q: How long after the storm can I file a hail damage claim in Texas?
faqs.a: Most Texas carriers give you one year from the date of loss to file. Practically, the sooner the better. Visible hail damage becomes harder to attribute to a specific storm as months pass and other weather events occur. If you suspect damage from a recent confirmed hail event in your Austin zip code, get an inspection within 60 days while the evidence is fresh.

faqs.q: What is a Class 4 impact-rated roof and does it save money in Texas?
faqs.a: Class 4 is the highest impact rating available for a roofing material. To earn it, the material has to survive a two-inch steel ball dropped from twenty feet without cracking. Every major Texas insurance carrier offers a ten to twenty percent discount on the wind and hail portion of the homeowners premium for Class 4 roofs. Standing seam metal, Decra stone-coated steel, most synthetic slates including DaVinci, and select Class 4 asphalt shingles like Owens Corning Duration Storm all qualify.

faqs.q: Will my insurance premium go up if I file a hail claim in Texas?
faqs.a: In Texas, single hail claims rarely cause a rate increase. Hail is considered a catastrophic peril and most carriers rate it into the policy at issue. Multiple claims in a short window can flag the policy for non-renewal at the next term. We walk you through this honestly before any claim is filed so you make the right call with full information.

faqs.q: Do you do storm chasing in Austin?
faqs.a: No. We are an Austin-based crew with twenty-seven years in Central Texas. No out-of-state trucks. No door-knocking. No salesmen with clipboards in your neighborhood claiming we were in the area doing a roof. You call us. We come out. That is the entire process.

name: Free Roof Inspection
h1: Free Roof Inspection in Austin, TX
card_summary: Forty-five minutes on the roof, every slope walked, attic checked from inside, written report emailed the same day. About thirty percent of our Austin free inspections conclude the homeowner does not need work right now. We tell you so.
direct_answer: Austin Area Roofers runs free roof inspections across the Austin metro within 48 hours of booking. The inspector walks every accessible slope, checks the attic from inside for active leaks and moisture, photographs every finding, and emails a written report the same day. No charge, no obligation, no upsell. About thirty percent of our Austin free inspections conclude the homeowner does not need any work right now and we tell them so.
intro_paragraphs: Most free roof inspections in Austin are sales pitches in disguise. Twenty-five minute walks, a few cherry-picked photos, and a written quote engineered to land on the same conclusion every time. Your roof needs replacing. Some of those conclusions are correct. Most are not. We have inspected behind enough of them to know.

Our free inspection is a real inspection. Forty-five minutes on average. Every accessible slope walked. Every penetration and flashing checked. Attic inspected from inside the home for active moisture, daylight visible through decking, animal entry, ventilation issues, and ceiling stain origins. Every finding photographed wide for context and close for evidence. Written report emailed the same day with our recommendation. Repair, replace, or nothing wrong and see you in five years.

About thirty percent of the Austin free inspections we run end with no work recommended. We tell the homeowner so, hand them a clean bill of health, and walk away. The other seventy percent get the same honesty about what is actually wrong. We are not optimizing for the close. We are optimizing for the call you make to us in three years when something does need fixing.

what_we_do.p: Flashings, valleys, penetrations, ridge caps, vents, soft metals, exposed fasteners, granule loss, and shingle bruising all inspected by foot. Nothing inspected through binoculars from the driveway.

Daylight visible through decking, active moisture staining, ceiling discoloration, ventilation, and animal entry all checked from inside before we leave the property. Most leaks tell their story in the attic before the ceiling stain shows up.
Every finding photographed wide for context and close for evidence. Filed with your inspection report so you have a record whether you act on it today or in three years.
Honest report in your inbox by end of day. Repair recommended with a quoted price, replacement recommended with a quoted range, or nothing wrong and see you in five years. With photos and notes.

If a roof is unsafe to walk because of pitch or condition, we use a drone with a 4K thermal camera. Every elevation photographed, every finding documented. We do not inspect by binocular from the driveway and call it complete.

process_steps.p: Pick a two-hour window that works. We confirm by text the morning of and call thirty minutes out so you are not waiting.

process_steps.h: Inspector arrives marked and on time
process_steps.p: Certified Austin Area Roofers inspector, marked truck, ID on the shirt. Forty-five minutes on average for a single-family Austin home.
Every accessible slope, every penetration, attic checked from inside. Photographs throughout.
We walk you through what we found before we leave the property. Verbal first, written follow-up same day.
Photos, findings, recommendation. Repair quote if needed. Replacement range if needed. Clean bill of health if not. No follow-up calls unless you ask us to.

faqs.q: Is the Austin Area Roofers free inspection actually free?
faqs.a: Yes. No charge, no obligation, no commitment of any kind. We inspect, document, and report. If you need work, we quote it. If you do not, we tell you so. About thirty percent of our Austin free inspections conclude no work is needed and we walk away with no follow-up.

faqs.q: How long does a roof inspection take in Austin?
faqs.a: Forty-five minutes on average for a single-family Austin home. Steep multi-elevation roofs add time. Complex Hill Country geometry can run sixty to ninety minutes. Commercial inspections run one to two hours. The attic check from inside the home is part of every inspection.

faqs.q: Should I get a roof inspection after every Austin hail or storm event?
faqs.a: Yes. Within 48 hours of a confirmed hail or wind event in your zip is the most useful timing for an inspection. The damage is fresh, the insurance window is open, and the photographic evidence is clean. Waiting six months drops the chance of a successful insurance claim significantly because the damage becomes harder to attribute to a specific event.

faqs.q: What if your inspector finds something the other roofer missed?
faqs.a: It happens regularly. We have built successful insurance claims off findings other roofers walked past. We have also told Austin homeowners that the hail damage another company quoted them on was actually wear consistent with age and would not pass an adjuster's inspection. Either way, we document our findings honestly and hand you the photos so you can decide what to do next.

faqs.q: Do you inspect commercial roofs in Austin?
faqs.a: Yes. Commercial standing seam, R-panel, modified bitumen, TPO, and EPDM systems all sit inside our standard inspection workflow. Commercial inspections run one to two hours depending on roof size and complexity. We provide written reports with photographic findings the same business day.

faqs.q: Will you sell me a roof I do not need?
faqs.a: No. We are not optimizing for the close. We are optimizing for the call you make to us in three years when something does need fixing. About thirty percent of our free inspections conclude no work is needed. We tell those homeowners so and walk away. That number is the entire reason we hold a perfect 5-star rating across every Google review we have ever received.

name: Commercial Roofing
card_summary: Light commercial roofs across the Austin metro. Modified bitumen, standing seam R-panel, TPO, EPDM, and built-up. Property managers, light industrial, retail, and small office. Same family-built crew, same hand-walked inspection standard.
direct_answer: Commercial roofing in Austin runs $8 to $14 per square foot for modified bitumen on flat decks, $11 to $18 per square foot for TPO and EPDM single-ply membrane, and $14 to $22 per square foot for R-panel and standing seam metal on light commercial buildings. Austin Area Roofers handles light commercial roofs across the Austin metro including warehouses, retail strips, small offices, multifamily, and property-managed portfolios. We coordinate with property managers, write transferable workmanship warranties, and minimize tenant disruption with off-hours and phased installs.
intro_paragraphs: Commercial roofs fail differently than residential roofs. Ponded water on a low-slope deck, seam separation on a single-ply membrane, blistering on a modified bitumen field, fastener back-out on an R-panel system, and curb flashings around RTUs that nobody has touched since the building was new. Twenty-seven years of Austin work means we know what fails first on each system and we price the replacement, restoration, or recover-over against the actual condition we see on the deck, not against a guess.

We install modified bitumen, TPO, EPDM, R-panel standing seam, and recover systems for property owners and managers across the Austin metro. Most of our commercial work sits in the 5,000 to 60,000 square foot range. Warehouses around Manor and Pflugerville, retail strips along South Lamar and East Riverside, small office and medical buildings in North Austin, and multifamily across the metro. We coordinate with property management, write transferable workmanship warranties, and run off-hours installs where the tenant base requires it.

Every commercial inspection includes core samples where the deck condition is unclear, infrared moisture scans on TPO and EPDM systems when the building file is missing prior repair history, and a written scope with photographs. We are bonded, insured, and registered with the State of Texas. References from local property managers available before any contract.

what_we_do.p: Two-ply SBS modified bitumen with cap sheet, hot-mopped or torch-applied to project conditions. Twenty-year manufacturer warranty registered in the building owner's name. Recover-over an existing system where the deck is sound and the original installation allows.

60-mil, 80-mil, or 90-mil TPO and EPDM systems mechanically attached or fully adhered. Hot-air-welded seams. Twenty to thirty year manufacturer warranty depending on membrane spec. Walk pads installed at every RTU and access point as part of the price.

26-gauge or 24-gauge R-panel and standing seam in Galvalume or Kynar-coated finishes. Concealed-fastener systems for the long-haul commercial install. Forty to fifty year manufacturer warranty. Class 4 impact rated where the spec calls for it.

what_we_do.h: RTU curbs, drains, scuppers, and pitch pans
what_we_do.p: The detail work that decides whether the roof leaks in year three or year twenty. Every RTU curb reflashed by hand. Drains rebuilt with new clamping rings. Scuppers replaced where the membrane has failed. Pitch pans poured with two-part urethane sealant, not the cheap stuff.

Annual and biannual maintenance contracts with documented inspection reports for the building file. Storm response within 24 hours for tenanted property. Emergency tarping and temporary patch service to prevent business interruption while the permanent fix gets scheduled.

process_steps.p: We meet you on the roof, walk every elevation, photograph every deficiency, and write the report the same business day. Free for buildings inside our 75-mile Austin service radius.

We give you two or three options where the building condition allows it. Repair-and-extend, recover-over, or full tear-off and replace. Each option priced separately so you can show ownership or finance the spread.
We coordinate with your property manager on tenant notice, parking lot staging, dumpster placement, and start times. Off-hours installs available where the tenant base requires it.
Daily photographic progress reports emailed to the property manager. Magnetic sweeps every evening. Site secured at end of each shift.

Manufacturer warranty registered in the building owner's name before the final invoice. Full as-built file with photographs, material certifications, and warranty documents handed to the property manager for the building binder.

faqs.q: What commercial roofing systems do you install in Austin?
faqs.a: Modified bitumen (SBS two-ply with cap sheet, hot-mopped or torch-applied), TPO and EPDM single-ply membrane (60, 80, and 90-mil thicknesses), R-panel and standing seam metal (26 and 24-gauge), and built-up gravel systems for owners who want to maintain a legacy roof. We coordinate with the building's structural engineer on dead load where the system change requires it.

faqs.q: How much does commercial roofing cost per square foot in Austin in 2026?
faqs.a: Modified bitumen runs $8 to $14 per square foot installed in the Austin metro. TPO and EPDM single-ply membrane runs $11 to $18 per square foot depending on attachment method and thickness. R-panel and standing seam metal runs $14 to $22 per square foot. Recover-over an existing system runs roughly 60 to 75 percent of the cost of a full tear-off and replace where the original deck and structure allow.

faqs.q: Do you work with commercial property managers and insurance carriers?
faqs.a: Yes. The majority of our commercial work comes through property managers and ownership groups across the Austin metro. We carry general liability and workers compensation, are bonded, and provide certificates of insurance to the property manager before any work begins. We also handle commercial insurance claims for hail and wind events directly with the carrier.

faqs.q: Can you replace a commercial roof without interrupting tenants?
faqs.a: Most of the time, yes. We run off-hours and phased installs on tenanted property. Modified bitumen and single-ply membrane work can be scheduled overnight or on weekends where the building owner authorizes the labor premium. Metal panel installs typically run during business hours because the noise envelope is tighter, but we phase the work by section so the tenant footprint stays open.

faqs.q: What warranty do you write on commercial work?
faqs.a: Manufacturer warranties run 20 years on modified bitumen, 20 to 30 years on TPO and EPDM depending on thickness, and 40 to 50 years on metal panel systems. Our written workmanship warranty runs five years on labor and is transferable to the next building owner. Both are registered in the owner's name and included in the as-built file.

faqs.q: Do you handle ongoing commercial roof maintenance?
faqs.a: Yes. We offer annual and biannual maintenance contracts with photographed inspection reports filed for the building binder. Coverage includes inspection, debris clearance, seam and flashing check, drain and scupper clearance, RTU curb inspection, and a written report with recommendations. Maintenance contracts often extend the usable life of a commercial system by 5 to 10 years.

name: Asphalt Shingle Roofing
h1: Asphalt Shingle Roofing in Austin, TX
card_summary: Architectural asphalt shingle, the workhorse Austin install. IKO Master tier, Owens Corning Preferred, GAF lines available on request. Class 4 impact rated lines for the Texas hail belt. Two-layer underlayment and six-nails-per-shingle high-wind builds standard on every job.
direct_answer: Architectural asphalt shingle roofing in Austin runs $8,000 to $18,000 installed on a typical 2,000 to 2,500 square foot home in 2026, with the average install landing at $12,000 to $14,000. Austin Area Roofers is IKO Master tier and Owens Corning Preferred, runs two-layer synthetic underlayment and six-nails-per-shingle high-wind specs on every job, and offers Class 4 impact-rated lines that qualify for a 10 to 20 percent discount on the wind-and-hail portion of most Texas homeowners insurance policies.
intro_paragraphs: Asphalt shingle is the workhorse Austin install. About sixty percent of the roofs we replace each year are architectural asphalt shingle. The reason is the cost-to-life ratio. A premium architectural shingle installed correctly will run 25 to 30 years in the Central Texas climate at a fraction of the cost of metal, tile, or synthetic slate. Installed incorrectly, the same shingle will fail at year 12 and the homeowner will blame the manufacturer when the actual problem was the underlayment, the nailing pattern, or the flashing.

We install IKO Cambridge and Dynasty, Owens Corning TruDefinition Duration and Duration Storm, and GAF Timberline HDZ and Armorshield II lines on request. Class 4 impact-rated lines on every line that supports it. Two-layer synthetic underlayment as the standard, not the upgrade. Six nails per shingle in the high-wind pattern on every job regardless of which Central Texas zip code the home sits in. The wind rating on a shingle is only as good as the nailing pattern that holds it down, and we hold the rating to the manufacturer spec on every install.

Color and line selection happens on your driveway with full sample boards in natural daylight. We do not show samples in a sales office under fluorescent light because the color reads different on the roof in Austin sun. We will tell you which lines to skip for your specific roof pitch, your existing siding color, and your HOA covenants. Twenty-seven years of Austin installs means we know which colors fade fastest under Hill Country sun and which lines have the actual track record to back the manufacturer warranty claim.

what_we_do.p: Old shingle and underlayment fully stripped to the deck. Every soft, rotten, or hail-bruised sheathing board photographed and replaced. We do not shingle over decking that will fail in five years and call it a new roof.

Synthetic underlayment as the standard on every elevation. Ice and water shield in valleys, around chimneys, around skylights, and on every penetration. Drip edge on every eave and rake. Texas code on every install.

IKO Nordic, Owens Corning Duration Storm, GAF Armorshield II, and Malarkey Vista AR. Each of these lines carries the UL 2218 Class 4 rating that qualifies the homeowner for a 10 to 20 percent discount on the wind-and-hail portion of most Texas insurance policies. We file the certification with your carrier so the discount actually shows up on your renewal.

Six nails per shingle in the high-wind pattern on every install. Standard nailing is four nails, which holds to 110 mph. Six nails holds to 130 mph and is required to register the manufacturer's high-wind warranty option. We hold the spec on every job regardless of whether the homeowner asked for it.

Step flashing along every chimney and sidewall, cut and folded on your driveway from continuous metal stock. Pipe boots, attic vents, and roof penetrations sealed with two-part urethane, not the cheap stuff. Crickets behind every chimney wider than 30 inches because the manufacturer warranty requires it.

process_steps.p: Sixty seconds online or a five-minute call. We size the roof from satellite imagery and price it within 8 percent of the on-site final before anyone steps on your property.
Full IKO, Owens Corning, GAF, and Malarkey sample boards on your driveway in natural daylight. We tell you which lines fit your roof pitch, siding color, and HOA covenants and which to skip.
City of Austin, Round Rock, Cedar Park, or whichever municipality applies. We pull the permit, schedule the install around your work week, and deliver materials the day before the crew arrives.

Most Austin asphalt shingle roofs install in one or two days with the same family-built crew start to finish. Two-layer underlayment, six-nail high-wind pattern, hand-built flashings, ridge vent installed to manufacturer spec.

process_steps.h: Magnetic sweep, warranty registration, two-year written workmanship warranty
process_steps.p: Final magnetic sweep across the lawn and beds. Manufacturer warranty registered in your name before the truck leaves. Ten-year written workmanship warranty on labor, transferable once to the next owner.

faqs.q: How much does asphalt shingle roof replacement cost in Austin in 2026?
faqs.a: Architectural asphalt shingle on a typical 2,000 to 2,500 square foot Austin home runs $8,000 to $18,000 installed, with the average landing at $12,000 to $14,000. Premium impact-rated lines run $1,500 to $3,000 above the standard architectural and pay back through insurance discount in roughly three to five years for most Austin homeowners.

faqs.q: How long does an asphalt shingle roof last in Austin?
faqs.a: A properly installed architectural asphalt shingle roof runs 25 to 30 years in the Central Texas climate. The premium lines such as IKO Crowne Slate and Owens Corning Duration Premium carry 30 to 50 year manufacturer warranties. The actual service life depends more on installation quality, attic ventilation, and hail exposure than on the shingle line itself.

faqs.q: What is a Class 4 impact-rated shingle and does it qualify for an insurance discount?
faqs.a: Class 4 is the highest UL 2218 impact rating, achieved by shingles that pass the 2-inch steel ball drop test from 20 feet without cracking. IKO Nordic, Owens Corning Duration Storm, GAF Armorshield II, and Malarkey Vista AR all carry the Class 4 rating. Most Texas homeowners insurance policies offer a 10 to 20 percent discount on the wind-and-hail portion of the premium when the home is roofed with a Class 4 line. We file the certification with your carrier so the discount actually shows up on renewal.

faqs.q: What is the difference between three-tab and architectural shingles?
faqs.a: Three-tab is the older flat-look single-layer shingle. Architectural (also called dimensional or laminate) shingle is the modern two-layer shingle with a thicker profile, higher wind rating, and the visual depth that reads as quality from the street. We install architectural on every job. Three-tab is no longer worth the labor savings and most Texas insurance carriers now decline the discount on three-tab installations.

faqs.q: Do you install IKO, Owens Corning, GAF, or all of them?
faqs.a: We install all of them. We are IKO Master tier and Owens Corning Preferred Contractor. GAF Timberline and Armorshield II are available on request. We will tell you which line fits your roof pitch, your siding color, your HOA covenants, and your insurance situation, and we will tell you which to skip. We do not sell up. We install what fits the house.

faqs.q: What warranty comes with an asphalt shingle roof?
faqs.a: Manufacturer warranties on architectural asphalt shingle run 25 to 50 years on materials depending on the line, with the premium lines carrying lifetime non-prorated coverage on the homeowner who registers the install. Our written workmanship warranty runs 10 years on labor and is transferable once to the next owner. Both are registered in your name before our truck leaves the property and emailed to you on close-out.

name: Flat Roof Replacement
h1: Flat Roof Replacement in Austin, TX
card_summary: Modified bitumen, TPO, and torch-down systems for the flat and low-slope roofs across the Austin metro. Pitch-and-gravel replacements done with hand-feathered torch down for a watertight seal. Same family-built crew, same warranty-registered install.
direct_answer: A flat roof replacement in Austin runs $8 to $14 per square foot for two-ply SBS modified bitumen, $11 to $18 per square foot for TPO single-ply membrane, and $9 to $15 per square foot for torch-down replacements of legacy pitch-and-gravel systems. Austin Area Roofers handles flat-roof additions on Hill Country modern builds, rear-house flat sections on 1960s-era Austin ranches, and low-slope porch and patio coverings across the metro.
intro_paragraphs: Flat roofs in Austin live and die by the seam work, the drainage, and the flashing details at every penetration. Twenty-seven years of work on Central Texas flat and low-slope roofs means we know what fails first on each system. Modified bitumen seams that were rolled cold instead of hand-torched. TPO membranes mechanically attached where the building required fully-adhered. Torch-down feathering rushed by a crew that did not understand the difference between hot enough to bond and hot enough to leak through.

We replace flat roofs in three systems. Two-ply SBS modified bitumen with cap sheet, hand-torched and feathered on every seam. TPO single-ply membrane in 60-mil, 80-mil, and 90-mil thicknesses, hot-air-welded at every overlap. Torch-down replacements of legacy pitch-and-gravel decks where the building owner wants modern weatherproofing without the asphalt and stone weight load. Most Austin flat-roof replacements we run fall in the $10,000 to $35,000 range for residential additions and small commercial sections.

Every flat-roof replacement starts with a deck inspection and core sample where the condition is unclear. We document drainage with a slope reading, photograph every penetration, and write the scope before any tear-off begins. Manufacturer warranty registered in your name before our truck leaves the property.

what_we_do.p: Base sheet and cap sheet, hot-mopped or hand-torched to project conditions. Feathered seams at every overlap so the bond holds and water cannot wick under the membrane. Twenty-year manufacturer warranty registered in your name.

60, 80, and 90-mil thicknesses available. Hot-air-welded seams at every overlap. Mechanically attached or fully adhered to building spec. Walk pads at every RTU and access point. 20 to 30 year manufacturer warranty depending on membrane spec.
Legacy pitch-and-gravel deck stripped to substrate. New base and cap sheet torch-applied and feathered by hand. Eliminates the gravel weight load and the maintenance overhead of the legacy system.

Drains rebuilt with new clamping rings. Scuppers replaced where the membrane has failed. Pitch pans poured with two-part urethane sealant. The detail work that decides whether the roof leaks in year three or year twenty.

Two-ply modified bitumen runs 20 years on materials. TPO runs 20 to 30 years. Our written workmanship warranty runs five years on labor, transferable once to the next owner. Both registered in your name before close-out.

process_steps.p: We meet you on the roof, walk every elevation, take a core sample where the deck condition is unclear, photograph every penetration. Free across the Austin metro.
Modified bitumen, TPO, or torch-down replacement. We price the options separately so you can choose what fits the building, the budget, and the long-term plan.
City of Austin or whichever municipality applies. Material delivered the day before the crew arrives. Dumpster placement coordinated with the property.
Old membrane stripped, deck inspected and replaced where it has failed. New base sheet laid, cap sheet torch-applied and feathered by hand. Drainage and flashings rebuilt at every penetration.
Final magnetic sweep across the lawn and beds. Manufacturer warranty registered in your name. Five-year written workmanship warranty on labor.

faqs.q: How much does flat roof replacement cost in Austin?
faqs.a: Modified bitumen runs $8 to $14 per square foot installed in the Austin metro. TPO single-ply membrane runs $11 to $18 per square foot depending on thickness and attachment method. Torch-down replacement of legacy pitch-and-gravel runs $9 to $15 per square foot. Most Austin residential flat-roof replacements fall in the $10,000 to $35,000 range.

faqs.q: What is the difference between torch-down and modified bitumen?
faqs.a: Modified bitumen is the membrane material itself. Torch-down is the installation method. Two-ply SBS modified bitumen can be installed by hot-mopping or by torch-application. Torch-down means the underside of the cap sheet is heated with an open flame and pressed into the base sheet, bonding the two layers as the asphalt melts. Done right, the seam is hand-feathered: hot enough to bond, not hot enough to leak through.

faqs.q: What is pitch and gravel and how do you replace it?
faqs.a: Pitch and gravel (also called built-up roofing or BUR) is the legacy commercial flat-roof system. Layers of asphalt-saturated felt are mopped down hot, then a layer of gravel is embedded into the final mop. Most Austin pitch-and-gravel roofs were installed in the 1960s to 1990s. We replace them with torch-down modified bitumen, which eliminates the gravel weight load and modernises the weatherproofing without changing the roof's structural assembly.

faqs.q: Can you replace a flat roof on a residential addition?
faqs.a: Yes. Residential flat-roof additions are the most common flat-roof replacement we run in Austin. Hill Country modern builds with flat sections, rear-of-house flat additions on 1960s ranches, and porch and patio covers all fall in our wheelhouse. The same family-built crew handles both the residential flat-roof work and the standard pitched-roof work, so the seam where they meet is built right.

faqs.q: How long does a flat roof last in Austin?
faqs.a: Modified bitumen runs 20 to 25 years in the Central Texas climate when installed correctly. TPO runs 20 to 30 years depending on thickness. Torch-down replacements over a sound deck run 20 years on materials. The actual service life depends more on drainage, flashing detail, and how the seams were sealed than on the membrane material itself.

faqs.q: Do you handle flat-roof commercial work?
faqs.a: Yes. About half of our commercial roofing work is flat-roof or low-slope. Modified bitumen, TPO, EPDM, and R-panel metal across warehouses, retail strips, and small office and medical buildings. See our commercial roofing page for the property-manager and ownership-group workflow.

name: Flat Roof Repair
h1: Flat Roof Repair in Austin, TX
card_summary: Flat roof leak diagnosis and repair across the Austin metro. Modified bitumen seam repair, TPO patch and weld, pitch-and-gravel torch-down section repairs. Two-year workmanship warranty written on every repair.
direct_answer: Most Austin flat roof repairs run $550 to $2,800 depending on access, system type, and the extent of the membrane failure. A modified bitumen seam re-torch runs $550 to $900. A TPO membrane patch with hot-air weld runs $700 to $1,400. A pitch-and-gravel section replacement with torch-down runs $900 to $2,200. Austin Area Roofers carries every repair with a two-year workmanship warranty written on close-out.
intro_paragraphs: Flat roof leaks rarely show up where the water actually enters the building. Water tracks along the underside of the deck, runs along a beam, or pools at the lowest point before it drops through the ceiling. Twenty-seven years of Central Texas flat-roof repair calls means we know how to trace the leak to the source before we quote the fix.

We repair every flat-roof system we install. Modified bitumen seam repair, TPO patch and weld, EPDM section replacement, and torch-down repair on legacy pitch-and-gravel. Most repairs we run in the Austin metro fall in the $550 to $2,800 range. About fifteen percent of the flat-roof repair calls we run end in our recommending a replacement instead, because the membrane is past the point where another patch throws good money after bad. We will tell you that straight.
Emergency tarp service available within 24 hours for active leaks during storm season. Two-year workmanship warranty written on every repair.

what_we_do.p: We trace the water path. Flat-roof leaks often track ten to twenty feet from the entry point before they show up on the ceiling. No guesswork, no roof reseal-everything pitch.
Failed seam stripped, fresh cap sheet torch-applied and hand-feathered. The bond holds because the seam is brought to exact temperature. Hot enough to fuse, not hot enough to leak through.
Punctured or split TPO membrane patched with matching material and hot-air-welded at every overlap. The weld is stronger than the parent membrane when done right.
Failed section of legacy pitch-and-gravel stripped, base and cap sheet torch-applied to the surrounding original system. Hand-feathered transition so the seam holds.

Clogged or back-pitched drains rebuilt. Failed scuppers replaced where the membrane has split around them. Pitch pans repoured. The detail work that decides whether the next storm leaks in the same spot.

process_steps.p: We trace the leak path on-roof and in the attic if accessible. Written scope with photographs the same business day. Free across the Austin metro.
Where the membrane condition allows it, we give you a repair option and a section-replacement option separately so you can decide what fits the building and budget.
Most flat-roof repairs schedule within the same week. Emergency tarp service available within 24 hours for active leaks during storm season.
Repair completed by the same family-built crew that handles our replacements. Before and after photos sent to your file before our truck leaves the property.
Two-year warranty on the workmanship of every flat-roof repair we run. If the same spot leaks again, we come back at no charge.

faqs.q: How much does flat roof repair cost in Austin?
faqs.a: Most Austin flat roof repairs run $550 to $2,800 depending on access, system type, and the extent of the membrane failure. A modified bitumen seam re-torch runs $550 to $900. A TPO membrane patch with hot-air weld runs $700 to $1,400. A pitch-and-gravel section replacement with torch-down runs $900 to $2,200.

faqs.q: Can you trace a flat roof leak before I commit to a repair?
faqs.a: Yes. About forty percent of the flat-roof calls we run end in the leak source being ten to twenty feet from where the water shows up on the ceiling. Water tracks along the underside of the deck and along beams before it drops through. We trace the path on-roof and in the attic before we quote the fix. The diagnostic itself is included in the free inspection.

faqs.q: Do you offer emergency tarp service for an active flat roof leak?
faqs.a: Yes. Emergency tarp service within 24 hours for active leaks during storm season. The tarp buys you a dry interior while we scope the permanent repair. Tarp service runs $350 to $750 depending on access and roof size, and credited toward the permanent fix if you book it with us.

faqs.q: What warranty do you write on flat roof repairs?
faqs.a: Two-year written workmanship warranty on every flat-roof repair we run. If the same spot leaks again within the warranty period, we come back at no charge. One of the few Austin crews that writes a multi-year workmanship warranty on flat-roof repair work.

faqs.q: When is a flat roof repair not worth doing?
faqs.a: About fifteen percent of the flat-roof repair calls we run end in our recommending a replacement instead. If the membrane is more than 80 percent of the way through its service life, if there are multiple seam failures across the field, or if the deck below has rot, the next dollar is better spent on a replacement than on another patch. We will tell you that straight.

faqs.q: Do you repair commercial flat roofs?
faqs.a: Yes. Commercial flat-roof repair is a regular part of our weekly workflow. Property managers and ownership groups across the Austin metro contract us for emergency repair, seasonal maintenance, and pre-replacement scope work. See our commercial roofing page for the property-manager workflow.
